2ND OPPOSITION TO NOTICE OF MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO FURNISH SECURITY FILED BY PLAINTIFF PICKENS, JANET

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOHN STEWART COMPANY'S MOTION FOR ORDER REQUIRING PLAINTIFF TO FURNISH SECURITY CCP 391.1 IS GRANTED. THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE COMPLAINT AND THE MOVING PAPERS SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANT PURSUANT TO MORAN V. MURTAUGH MILLER MEYER & NELSON, LLP (2007) 40 CAL. 4TH 780. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NO PROBABILITY OF SUCCESS IN THIS ACTION IS REQUIRED TO POST A BOND OF $500.00. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Pltf To Furnish Security Ccp 391.1;( P/A; Request For Judicial Notice; Declaration) Ccp 128.7
Off Calendar Aug-07-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Paul Arshawsky of Zanghi Torres Arshawsky (977.0444) for defendant and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JOHN STEWART COMPANY'S MOTION FOR ORDER REQUIRING PLAINTIFF TO FURNISH SECURITY CCP 391.1 is granted. The Court has reviewed the complaint and the moving papers submitted by defendant pursuant to Moran v. Murtaugh Miller Meyer & Nelson, LLP (2007) 40 Cal. 4th 780. The Court finds that plaintiff has no probability of success in this action is required to post a bond of $500.00. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
